---
title: "French Revolution / Jacobin Republic"
---

::: {.callout-note title="Status: draft corpus"}
This case has source text, manifest registration, segmentation, and MIPVU
worklists, but no reviewed annotations, support rating, or reliability sample
yet. French source text is annotated with English glosses as analytical aids.
:::

## Analytical focus

The French Revolution case examines Robespierre-centered Jacobin rhetoric about
republican virtue, terror, public safety, sacred peoplehood, civic obligation,
and the moral-religious framing of revolutionary festivals.

## Corpus boundary

This is a compact Jacobin public corpus, not a general French Revolution
archive. The initial draft includes two Robespierre Convention reports from
1794, selected because the pre-v1 expansion review identified them as a
high-value French-source test for virtue, terror, sacred republican objects,
and sacrifice language.

## Draft artifacts

- [Corpus analysis](../../analysis/corpus-analysis.md)
- [Diachronic analysis](../../analysis/diachronic-analysis.md)
- [Metaphor-system evolution](../../analysis/cluster-evolution.md)
- [Critical metaphor analysis](../../analysis/critical-metaphor-analysis.md)
- [Rhetorical and genre analysis](../../analysis/rhetorical-genre-analysis.md)
- [Absence and agency analysis](../../analysis/absence-agency-analysis.md)

Return to [all cases](../../../../case-selection.qmd).
